title: 'Netanyahu is driving Israel further away from its European and American allies than ever before'
subhead: The more wars Israel wages, the more isolated the country becomes, writes Le Monde's Gilles Paris in his column.

UK and France strike new Channel migration deal with boosted enforcement and funding
subhead: France will increase coastal policing by more than half, while Britain is pledging up to 766 million euros in funding.

title: Iran refuses to open Strait of Hormuz as long as US blockade remains in place
subhead: 'A complete ceasefire only has meaning if it is not violated through a naval blockade,' said Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who led Tehran's delegation in the first round of talks in Islamabad.

title: The playful world of British painter Rose Wylie exhibited for the first time in France
subhead: The 91-year-old British painter, who has become recognized late in her career and is known for her deceptively naïve style, is holding her first solo show in France at the Zwirner Gallery in Paris. There, she pays tribute to the French artist Le Douanier Rousseau, reinterpreting his work "Mauvaise surprise" ("Unpleasant Surprise").

title: Sealing the internet: How Putin wants to cut Russia off from the rest of the world
subhead: Controlling information has become a matter of survival for the Russian leader. Since 2019, he has sought to create a 'sovereign RuNet' under his control and cut off from the world. The technical implementation of this project has accelerated since the start of the war in Ukraine in 2022.

subhead: The US president has not set a new deadline for Iran, instead waiting for the various political and security factions within the Iranian regime to reach a 'unified proposal.'

title: Ireland, the weak link in Europe's defense
subhead: Irish neutrality, long seen as a guarantee of independence from its former British colonizer, has been eroding under European and Atlantic pressures. After decades of naval weakness, Dublin has committed to strengthening its navy.

title: Louvre showcases 'beautiful unknown' Martin Schongauer, who influenced Michelangelo and Rembrandt
subhead: An outstanding exhibition brings together around 100 works by the engraver and painter, who was born in Colmar around 1445, all showcasing his exceptional mastery.
